preamble: The shift from the voiced velar stop [g] to the high-front vowel [i] is a form of vocalization. In Germanic languages, this occurred when [g] (functioning as a palatal fricative [j] near front vowels) weakened and merged with the preceding vowel.

examples.note: Vocalization of the palatal fricative into the vowel system.

sources: Baugh & Cable: A History of the English Language
